title: 10 best herbal tinctures for red eyes

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tinctures helps with red eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal extract contains flavonoids, which are natural antioxidants that soothe irritated tissues and reduce swelling in the eyes. When applied topically or consumed as a supplement, eyebright tinctures help to calm redness and itchiness caused by allergies, dry air, or fatigue, promoting clear vision and comfort. By reducing inflammation, eyebright tinctures also alleviate dry eye symptoms, leaving your peepers feeling refreshed and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tincture contains compounds that reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, which helps to alleviate redness and discomfort caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis.
Antioxidant: Eyebright's antioxidant properties help to protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to age-related vision problems and eye fatigue.

Vasoconstrictive: The tannins in eyebright have a vasoconstrictive effect, causing blood vessels in the eyes to constrict and reducing blood flow to the affected area, which helps to relieve redness and itching caused by conditions such as allergic conjunctivitis.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Euphrasia officinalis tinctures contain phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce the inflammation and oxidative stress contributing to red eyes.

Alkaloids: The alkaloids present in Euphrasia officinalis tinctures, such as euphrasine and euphorine, exhibit vasoconstrictive and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce the redness and swelling associated with red eyes.

Terpenoids (specifically, iridoid glycosides): Iridoid glycosides, a type of terpenoid found in Euphrasia officinalis, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the eyes, reducing redness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm red eyes.

Flowers: They are used because they contain iridoid glycosides, which have been shown to have vasoconstrictive properties that can help reduce blood flow to the eyes, alleviating redness and irritation.
Stems: They are used due to their content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, similar to the leaves, which provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that help to calm and soothe red eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the aerial parts of euphrasia officinalis when in full bloom and at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested euphrasia officinalis aerial parts in a warm well-ventilated area for 2 to 4 weeks.
Combine 1 part of dried euphrasia officinalis aerial parts with 2 parts of 80 proof ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and label it appropriately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with red eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains a wealth of antioxidants, flavonoids, and carotenoids that work together to soothe and calm irritated eyes. When applied topically or taken internally as a supplement, pot marigold tincture can reduce swelling and ease discomfort caused by allergies, dryness, or other eye irritants. Its natural anti-inflammatory action also helps to reduce redness and puffiness, leaving eyes feeling cool, calm, and clear.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold contains flavonoids and carotenoids that reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate redness and discomfort.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tinctures help to neutralize free radicals that can damage eye tissues and contribute to redness, dryness, and irritation.
Soother of mucous membranes: The herb's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects also help to soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the eyes, reducing redness and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling associated with irritated eyes.
Phenolic acids (e.g., kaempferol, quercetin): As potent antioxidants, they can help mitigate oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, thereby reducing redness and discomfort.

Flavonoids (e.g., flavone, flavonol): These compounds possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the most commonly used part for tinctures due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and relieve red, irritated eyes.
Leaves: The leaves are also used in tinctures to reduce redness and inflammation in the eyes, thanks to their high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds.
Seeds: The seeds contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate red, itchy, and irritated eyes, making them a useful addition to eye tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers at peak bloom in late morning or early afternoon when essential oils are most concentrated.
Dry the flowers in a single layer in a well-ventilated area for 1 to 2 weeks to prevent mold.
Combine 1 part dried calendula flowers with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal tinctures helps with red eyes because it contains berberine, a potent natural antibacterial agent that targets the underlying causes of redness and inflammation. When used topically or taken internally as a supplement, goldenseal's antimicrobial properties help to reduce bacterial growth in the eye, thereby alleviating symptoms such as itching, burning, and excessive tearing. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm irritated eyes, promoting a sense of comfort and relief from redness.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Goldenseal's berberine content has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes by targeting underlying infections or irritations.

Alkaloids: The alkaloid compounds present in goldenseal tincture, including berberine and hydrastine, have a natural vasoconstrictive effect, which can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce redness in the eyes.
Antioxidants: Goldenseal's antioxidants can help protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic eye inflammation and redness.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Helps reduce redness and inflammation in the eyes by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
Hydrastine: Exhibits vasoconstrictive properties, which help constrict blood vessels in the eyes and reduce redness and swelling.
Hydrastis glycosides: Possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help prevent infections and reduce redness and inflammation in the eyes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used for making tinctures due to their high concentration of berberine, a compound that has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves contain a higher amount of hydrastine, a compound that has astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps in reducing redness and inflammation in the eyes.
Barks: The barks of the plant also contain berberine and hydrastine, which are used to make tinctures that can help alleviate redness, itching, and irritation in the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh hydrastis canadensis roots in the spring or fall with permission.
Clean the roots by gently scrubbing them with water to remove dirt and debris thoroughly.
Chop 1-2 cups of the cleaned roots into small pieces and combine them with 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every day or two.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids completely.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with red eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb, Echinacea pallida, is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that work together to reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes. This soothing effect helps alleviate redness, itchiness, and dryness associated with eye allergies, irritation, or fatigue. Additionally, Kansas coneflower's antimicrobial properties may help combat infections that can cause red eyes, providing fast and effective relief from this common annoyance.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like saponins and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the eyes, alleviating redness and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Kansas coneflower tincture has antioxidant properties that can neutralize free radicals and protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which may contribute to red eye conditions like dryness or irritation.

Anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds: The tincture contains compounds like caffeic acid and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial infections or allergies that may cause red eyes.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the redness and inflammation associated with irritated eyes.
Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, which can help calm and reduce redness in the eyes.
Flavonoids: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the eyes from damage and reduce redness caused by free radicals and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Echinacea angustifolia are commonly used in tinctures for red eyes due to their high concentration of alkaloids, particularly echinacoside, which has anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Echinacea angustifolia are used in tinctures for red eyes due to their content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.

Flowers: The flowers of Echinacea angustifolia are used in tinctures for red eyes due to their high levels of flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating effects.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried echinacea angustifolia roots and flowers in a glass container.
Combine the plant material with 2 parts of 80% ethanol in a separate container.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid from the plant material using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container.
Store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid to preserve its potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tinctures helps with red eyes because its natural anti-inflammatory properties effectively reduce swelling and irritation. The tincture's active compounds, such as achillein and chamazulene, work to soothe and calm the delicate tissues of the eye, reducing redness and discomfort. Additionally, yarrow's antimicrobial properties help to combat underlying infections or irritants that may be contributing to red eyes. As a result, herbal yarrow tinctures provide fast and effective relief from red, itchy, and irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and alleviate redness in the eyes by reducing prostaglandins and leukotrienes, which are involved in inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: Yarrow tincture's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to red eye symptoms such as dryness, itchiness, and irritation.

Astringent and antiseptic properties: Yarrow tincture's astringent and antiseptic compounds help reduce discharge and inflammation in the eyes, while also inhibiting the growth of bacteria and other pathogens that may be contributing to red eye symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, helping to reduce swelling and redness associated with eye irritation.
Luteolin: A flavonoid with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, luteolin can help alleviate redness and discomfort in the eyes by neutralizing free radicals and reducing inflammation.
Nootkatone: A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, nootkatone can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Achillea millefolium are used to make tinctures for red eyes due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Flowers: The flowers of Achillea millefolium are used to make tinctures for red eyes due to their antiseptic and soothing properties.
Roots: The roots of Achillea millefolium are used to make tinctures for red eyes due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh flowering tops of the plant in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase its surface area.
Combine 1 part of the chopped plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with red eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains flavonoids, which are natural antioxidants that reduce inflammation and swelling in the blood vessels of the eye. remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are primarily used for tinctures for red eyes because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Glycyrrhiza glabra leaves are used due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce redness and irritation in the eyes.

remedy_recipe: Gather 500g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and 750ml of vodka at 40% abv.
Crush the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the root powder with vodka in a clean glass jar at a 1:2 ratio.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce puffiness and irritation. The herbal remedy contains bioactive compounds like flavonoids and triterpenes that work to soothe and calm the delicate eye tissue. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the spring or fall season.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and combine it with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio.
Store the mixture in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to small dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 5 years.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots of echinacea purpurea in late summer or early fall when the plants are in bloom.

Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discard the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gelsemium sempervirens leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Chop the harvested leaves into small pieces and combine with 25% glycerin in a glass jar.
Add 375ml of 80-proof vodka to the jar and stir gently to mix well.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 weeks to allow maceration and infusion.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding solids and filter with coffee filter.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ribes nigrum berries in mid-summer when they are ripe.
Dry the fresh berries using a food dehydrator or air drying method for 3 days.
Crush dried ribes nigrum berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix one part of the crushed ribes nigrum with three parts of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and let it sit for 2 weeks.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality urtica dioica leaves and stems for optimal potency.
Cleanse the plant material by rinsing it in cold water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine 250 grams of the cleaned plant material with 1000 milliliters of 80% ethanol and let steep for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle to separate solids from liquids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 6 months before using as a remedy for red eyes.

Cleanly chop 50g of the harvested material into smaller pieces to facilitate solvent interaction.

Store the mixture at room temperature away from direct sunlight and heat for 14 days allowing for extraction.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of centaurea cyanus in quantities of 20% by weight relative to the solvent ethanol.
Combine the dried flowers with ethanol 60% by volume at a ratio of 1:3 for 30 minutes of maceration.
Steep the mixture in darkness for 14 days at room temperature without agitation or heat application.
Filter the tincture through cheesecloth into glass containers and store it in a cool dark place for 24 hours.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and label them with dosages of 20-40 drops sublingually 3 times daily.

Combine the chopped flowers with 250ml of high-proof vodka or rum in a clean glass jar, ensuring complete coverage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with red eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality matricaria chamomilla flowers in the early morning dew, collecting at least one pound of fresh material.
Clean and prepare the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for tincture making by removing any debris or stems.
Combine the prepared matricaria chamomilla flowers with 100 proof vodka at a ratio of one part herbs to three parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and allow it to steep for four weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another glass container, discarding the solids and labeling the finished product.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried valeriana officinalis roots and stalks measuring 20-25 grams.
Combine the dried botanicals with 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar at ratio 1:4.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for red eyes are natural remedies derived from plants that help alleviate and soothe irritated, inflamed, and red eyes. They work by reducing inflammation, improving eye health, and providing relief from discomfort. The best herbal tinctures for red eyes include Euphrasia officinalis, Calendula officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, Symphytum officinale, Aloe barbadensis, Hypericum perforatum, Achillea millefolium, Ginkgo biloba, Aloe vera, and Vaccinium macrocarpon. Without these remedies, red eyes can lead to persistent discomfort, blurred vision, and decreased productivity, ultimately impacting daily life and causing stress, fatigue, and decreased quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to red eyes that are treated with herbal tinctures are conjunctivitis, pink eye, and other inflammatory conditions affecting the eyes or surrounding skin. Herbal remedies like echinacea, goldenseal, and eyebright may help soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Other common issues treated with herbal tinctures include sinus infections, cold sores, and minor wounds. These natural solutions often provide gentle, non-irritating relief without harsh chemicals or side effects.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with red eyes is a blend of Eyebright (Euphrasia officinalis) and Calendula (Calendula officinalis) tinctures. Eyebright is known for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to reduce swelling and combat infections. Calendula, on the other hand, soothes and calms the irritated eyes, reducing redness and promoting healing. Mixing equal parts of these tinctures with water and applying them to a clean cloth can provide quick relief from red, itchy eyes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Euphrasia officinalis tinctures helps with red eyes because it is known to reduce inflammation and alleviate congestion in the eyes. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties soothe the delicate tissues surrounding the eyes, calming redness and irritation. Additionally, Euphrasia officinalis is rich in antioxidants, which help to shield the eyes from environmental stressors and promote overall eye health. Regular use of Euphrasia officinalis tinctures may also help to maintain the health and resilience of the eyes, leading to a reduction in redness and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: They help with red eyes by reducing inflammation and soothing the mucous membranes in the eyes.
Phenylethanoid glycosides: They help by reducing redness and swelling due to their vasoconstrictive properties, which decrease blood flow to the affected area.
Flavonoids: They help by inhibiting the release of histamine and other chemical mediators that cause blood vessels to dilate, leading to redness and swelling.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make tinctures due to their high content of iridoid glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the eyes.

Flowers: They are used to make tinctures due to their high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes.

Roots: They are used to make tinctures due to their high content of iridoid glycosides and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and protect the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest euphrasia officinalis flowers in the early morning after the dew has evaporated and before the heat of the sun.
Clean the euphrasia officinalis flowers thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris from the harvest.
Combine 100 grams of euphrasia officinalis flowers with 500 milliliters of 35 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 14 days in a cool dark place with occasional gentle shaking of the jar.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Roots: The roots of Calendula officinalis are used to make tinctures for red eyes because they contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of calendula officinalis flowers when in full bloom for optimal potency and flavor.
Chop 1 cup of the fresh flowers into small pieces to increase the surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped flowers with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids.

Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.

Steep 1 part of chopped plant material in 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar for 6 weeks.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids after 6 weeks.

Wash the leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and soak them in 80 proof vodka at a ratio of 1 part aloe to 2 parts vodka for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids and transfer to a dark glass bottle.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks before using allowing the flavors to mature fully.

Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking it daily for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium tinctures helps with red eyes because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest the aerial parts of achillea millefolium at peak flowering stage when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place or using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 2-3 hours.
Combine 1 part dried plant material with 2 parts 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar to create the tincture.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks with occasional shaking of the jar to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to separate the liquid from the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers in late summer or early fall when they are fully ripe.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material in a warm place protected from direct sunlight for 2 days.
Combine 1 part dried ginkgo biloba leaves and flowers with 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and discard the solids.

Combine 1 pound of dried vaccinium macrocarpon with 2 cups of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
